author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
mvp-sync (`54caedd`) closed (2026-06-22). Three quality-tightening
child tickets shipped in a single afternoon arc:

- `1c20aa3` — 34 resolver unit tests (`reduce_to_merged` extracted as
  pure folder). Commit `527d759`.
- `004dd23` — dual-file path filter pinned as load-bearing with
  regression test. Commit `9bdc6b1`.
- `0896e4f` — `touched_by_merge` guard removed; surfaced a real
  `replay_ops` ordering divergence; fixed by sharing the resolver's
  `OpKey` via `sort_entries_lww`. Commit `a46581a`.

267 workspace tests green (was 231). Three mvp-storage / mvp-cli /
agent-ergonomics epics still above the line; mvp-sync now off the
roadmap and `multi-client` (which it blocked) becomes unblocked
for whenever it gets pulled above the line.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
New epic filed 2026-06-23: `ec5a0a8 epic:host-asterinas` —
host the asterinas-workspace project (~968 markdown-file
issues) on jjforge. Two children filed under it:

- `1588625 host-asterinas-audit` (research) — walk the
  asterinas tree end-to-end, compare against beads
  features, identify gaps. Output is a doc + follow-up
  feature tickets per gap.
- `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate` (feature) — Haiku
  fan-out import. Blocked by the audit.

Direction (user 2026-06-23): bend asterinas to fit
beads-style features in jjforge, not the other way
around. The audit identifies which beads primitives are
worth adding; we ship those first; then migrate.

The two parent-child edges are the first real test of
`b6d066b agent-dep-types`. `jjf dep tree ec5a0a8`
shows the hierarchy; `jjf ready --label epic:host-asterinas`
correctly returns the audit only (migrate is blocked).

This epic is below the line on the roadmap above for now;
treat it as test-bed for the agent-ergonomics primitives
we just shipped. It can be promoted above the line when
the audit lands.

Today's earlier shipping arc (`agent-remember`,
`agent-claim-atomic`, `agent-dep-types`, `agent-await-gates`
research → `Status::Blocked` impl, `storage-scale-index`
research → snapshot cache impl) is closed. 387 workspace
tests green; `jjf ready` warm = 52ms (was ~1.1s).

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body: Promoted epic:multi-project-readiness (52ee82d) to position 1 above epic:agent-ergonomics, after the 2026-06-24 QA pass surfaced four CRITICAL multi-machine sync bugs (F-001/F-002/F-003/F-010 — same root cause cluster around refs/jjf/meta/*). Bugs-before-features.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body: epic:multi-project-readiness closed 2026-06-25. Agent-ergonomics is now the top priority.
